title: puccinellia tenella subsp. langeana
intro: Puccinellia tenella subsp. langeana, also known as Lange's alkali grass, is a plant species that belongs to the grass family (Poaceae) and is native to the Arctic tundra regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Puccinellia tenella subsp. langeana include its potential to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using its roots in a decoction, which can be used to treat various health ailments.
